Here's everything we built, the numbers behind it, and what Month 2 looks like. ↓
caption_full:
One month in.
Here's everything we built, the numbers behind it, and what Month 2 looks like. ↓

Follow along. Month 2 starts Monday.

#SyncMaster #AfricanComposers #SyncLicensing #MusicBusiness #BuildingInPublic

slides.eyebrow: Behind the Scenes · Month 1 in numbers
slides.headline: One month in.
Here's everything we built, the numbers behind it, and what Month 2 looks like.

slides.lede: 47 tracks. 100% with ISRCs. 100% one-stop cleared. 100% stems available.

slides.headline:
THE CONTENT
42 pieces of content across 5 platforms in 30 days.
Here's what you need to know:
slides.lede: WHAT WE LEARNED · Speed wins. Every brief we didn't win was lost on response time, not quality. · Rights is the highest barrier. 9 of 14 rejected applications had rights issues. · Supervisors want African music. Genuinely. The demand is real and explicit.
list_items: Speed wins. Every brief we didn't win was lost on response time, not quality.
Rights is the highest barrier. 9 of 14 rejected applications had rights issues.
Supervisors want African music. Genuinely. The demand is real and explicit.
The brief-to-placement cycle is faster than we expected.
Building in public works. Application volume doubled in Week 3.

slides.headline:
MONTH 2
More composers.
slides.lede:
More briefs. More placements.
More content to prove it's real.

slides.headline: Open briefs. Every Tuesday.
slides.lede: Composers: apply once, get matched for life. Supervisors: shortlist in 48h.
